name: Christopher Hogwood
abstract: Christopher Jarvis Haley Hogwood  (10 September 1941 – 24 September 2014) was an English conductor, harpsichordist, and musicologist. Founder of the early music ensemble the Academy of Ancient Music, he was an authority on historically informed performance and a leading figure in the early music revival of the late 20th century.

about_quotes.speaker: Percy Heath
about_quotes.evidence: Connie had a stroke on the day we were supposed to go to Boston and play with these old instruments of this Bach Brandenburg Concerto... Hogwood said why don’t we do it without the drums, and we did.
about_quotes.doc_title: Percy Heath — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History
